topic: Skill-advisor scorer parent-hub compatibility for .opencode/specs/system-skill-advisor/016-skill-advisor-tuning and the mcp_server/lib/scorer code it documents. READ-ONLY proposals only; never edit advisor/scorer code.

focus: Angle 1: Layer-1b half-landed parent-hub review vocabulary
noveltyJustification: First pass established the primary metadata/scorer mismatch and the deletion/reindex dependency chain.
keyQuestions: Which deep-loop review terms still collide with sk-code code-review vocabulary?
answeredQuestions: Deep-loop metadata and registry still carry code-audit/severity terms while sk-code and explicit boosts now own single-pass review wording.

ruledOut.approach: Delete all review-loop vocabulary from deep-loop-workflows
ruledOut.reason: Deep-loop still needs iterative review-loop aliases; only single-pass code-audit wording is misplaced.

focus: Angle 2: Vocabulary authority split between hardcoded scorer boosts and metadata projection
noveltyJustification: New contract separates reindex-refreshable hub vocabulary from frozen code-only guardrails.
keyQuestions: Which vocabulary belongs in graph metadata versus scorer constants?
answeredQuestions: Hub and mode aliases belong in graph metadata; command bridges, task-class floors, and safety/abstain guards can remain code.

ruledOut.approach: Move all TOKEN_BOOSTS and PHRASE_BOOSTS into metadata immediately
ruledOut.reason: Some boosts encode non-skill policy and command bridge behavior, not parent-hub vocabulary.

noveltyJustification: The existing guard is reusable but only analyzes one hub at a time; the cross-hub owner dimension is absent.
keyQuestions: How should shared normalized phrases map to owning hubs and intent classes?
answeredQuestions: A cross-hub report can reuse the hub-local normalizer and classify collisions by intent class instead of treating every shared word as an error.

ruledOut.approach: Fail every cross-hub normalized phrase shared by two hubs
ruledOut.reason: Generic words like review/audit/design are valid across hubs unless they share the same intent class and route surface.

noveltyJustification: The guard validates trigger coverage but not every projected field the scorer actually consumes.
keyQuestions: Which typed aliases can be present in hub/router files but absent from advisor projection?
answeredQuestions: The scorer consumes intent_signals, derived.trigger_phrases, derived.key_topics/entities/key_files/source_docs, and doc triggers; parent-hub-vocab-sync only measures a subset.

ruledOut.reason: Projection also scores intentSignals and derivedKeywords, so trigger-only coverage misses typed-but-unprojected aliases.

focus: Angle 5: Ambiguous cross-hub prompt measurement set
noveltyJustification: Connected the existing ambiguity baseline to a missing labeled cross-hub fixture family.
keyQuestions: How should parent-hub compatibility be measured on ambiguous prompts?
answeredQuestions: Existing ambiguity gates are top-1 over low-margin or synthetic ties; they do not label cross-hub ambiguity semantics. A new hand-labeled fixture slice is needed.

ruledOut.approach: Reuse advisor_validate ambiguity_slice_stable as an ambiguity FP/FN metric
ruledOut.reason: The handler explicitly documents that it is only a synthetic stability check, not an empirical ambiguity-rate slice.

focus: Angle 6: Atomic reindex, re-baseline, ratchet recapture runbook
noveltyJustification: The runbook became concrete by tying projection fallback, SQLite fragility, and frozen baseline fixture pins together.
keyQuestions: What operation sequence avoids stale DB and stale ratchet results after metadata changes?
answeredQuestions: The safe sequence is metadata patch, skill graph scan/rebuild, advisor projection health, validate, baseline recapture, then ratchet review as one atomic evidence bundle.

ruledOut.approach: Patch graph-metadata and rely on filesystem fallback for validation
ruledOut.reason: Filesystem fallback is a degraded path after SQLite read failure and should not be treated as the authoritative post-reindex state.

noveltyJustification: The graph-causal conflict lane is live in code but unused in metadata, so criteria and measurement gates are the missing piece.
keyQuestions: Should parent-hub competitors be encoded as conflicts_with edges?
answeredQuestions: Use conflicts_with only for measured mutually-exclusive routing pairs with clear negative evidence; do not use it as broad taxonomy documentation.

ruledOut.approach: Add conflicts between all broad peer hubs
ruledOut.reason: All live skill conflict arrays are empty and the edge multiplier is negative; broad conflicts would create noisy demotion without measured pair evidence.

focus: Angle 8: COMMAND_BRIDGES hardcoding vs metadata-derived command bridges
noveltyJustification: Executor delegation provides a local precedent for metadata-derived routing tables that command bridges can copy.
keyQuestions: How can command bridge projections stop being hardcoded without adding hot-path coupling?
answeredQuestions: Derive bridges at build/reindex time from command contract metadata, then append projected command nodes the same way projection appends current hardcoded bridges.

ruledOut.approach: Read every command markdown file on each scoreAdvisorPrompt call
ruledOut.reason: Projection already separates load-time projection from scoring; hot-path command-file IO would add avoidable latency and coupling.

focus: Angle 9: Query-class, hub-router, and eval-bucket taxonomy mapping
noveltyJustification: The three taxonomies are now mapped to a validation target rather than treated as independent labels.
keyQuestions: Should query-class, hub-router classes, and eval buckets derive from each other?
answeredQuestions: They should not fully derive from one another, but they need a crosswalk fixture that proves every query-class bucket has expected hub-router ownership and eval coverage.

ruledOut.approach: Make classifyAdvisorQuery read every hub-router vocabulary class at runtime
ruledOut.reason: Query-class routing is a scorer-local coarse lane-weight feature; hub routers own mode selection after the top-level skill is chosen.

focus: Angle 10: semantic_shadow embedding hygiene for mcp-* near-neighbor attractors
noveltyJustification: The final pass reframed semantic_shadow work as hygiene/fixture isolation rather than lane-weight tuning.
keyQuestions: What is the cheapest response to mcp-chrome-devtools abstain false-fires?
answeredQuestions: Keep the frozen weight and improve description/embedding hygiene plus an mcp-neighbor abstain fixture; do not raise semantic weight.

ruledOut.approach: Increase semantic_shadow weight to improve recall
ruledOut.reason: The real-embedding ablation was net-negative and its harmful flips were gold-none abstain false-fires to mcp-chrome-devtools.
